qudipy.qutils package¶

Submodules¶

qudipy.qutils.math module¶

qudipy.qutils.math.inner_prod(gparams, wf1, wf2)[source]¶

Evaluates the inner product between two complex wavefunctions.

gparamsGridParameters class

Contains grid and potential information.

wf1complex array

‘Bra’ wavefunction for the inner product. If grid is 2D, then the array should be in meshgrid format.

wf2complex array

‘Ket’ wavefunction for the inner product. If grid is 2D, then the array should be in meshgrid format.

None.

inn_prodcomplex float

The inner product between the two inputted wavefunctions.

qudipy.qutils.math.partial_trace(rho, elem)[source]¶

Finds partial trace with respect to the specified qubit(s)

rho2D array

matrix of the dimensions 2**N x 2**N (density matrix in our case)

elemint / iterable of ints

number(s) of the electron(s) whose state(s) is/are averaged out

traced_rho: complex 2D array

new density matrix of the dimensions 2**m x 2**m, m = N - dim(elem)

qudipy.qutils.math.project_up(rho, elem)[source]¶

Projects the system density matrix onto the spin-up state of the specified electron(s) (without renormalizing it)

rho2D complex array

Matrix of the dimensions 2**N x 2**N (density matrix in our case).

elemint / iterable of ints

Number(s) of the electron(s) whose state(s) is to project the system density matrix on.

None.

projected_rhocomplex 2D array

new density matrix (non-renormalized) of the dimensions 2**m x 2**m, where m = N - dim(elem)

qudipy.qutils.math.project_down(rho, elem)[source]¶

Projects the system density matrix onto the spin-down state of the specified electron(s) (without renormalizing it).

rho2D array

Matrix of the dimensions 2**N x 2**N (density matrix in our case).

elemint / iterable of ints

Number(s) of the electron(s) whose state(s) is to project the system density matrix on.

None.

projected_rhocomplex 2D array

new density matrix (non-renormalized) of the dimensions 2**m x 2**m, where m = N - dim(elem)

qudipy.qutils.math.matrix_sqrt(A)[source]¶

Calculates a square root of the specified matrix.

Anumpy array

matrix to calculate the square root of

sqrt_A: complex 2D array

square root of the matrix

qudipy.qutils.math.partial_trace_general(rho, dim, traced_subsystem)[source]¶

This code takes the partial trace of a density matrix. It is adapted from the open-source TrX file written by Toby Cubitt for MATLAB.

rhocomplex 2D array

A 2D array describing a density matrix.

dim1D integer array

An array of the dimensions of each subsystem for the whole system for psi. [2,4,2] corresponds to a system of size 2x4x2 with 3 subsystems.

traced_subsystem1D array

An array of the subsystems to trace out. For dim=[2,4,2], sys=[1,3] would trace out the first and third subsystems leaving only a subspace with size 4.

None.

traced_rhocomplex 2D array

The resulting rho after taking the partial trace.

qudipy.qutils.matrices module¶

Constant matrices used to define quantum gates @author: hromecB, zmerino

class qudipy.qutils.matrices.Operator(operators=None, filename=None, f_type=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Initialize Operator class. Handles all things realted to creating/loading/modifying operator object files which contain various operators in dictionary format. The Operator class also contains hard coded operators.

CNOT(N, ctrl, trgt, save=False)[source]¶

Defines a matrix for CNOT gate.

Nint

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

ctrl: int

Index of the control qubit.

trgt: int

Index of the target qubit.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

None. Returns ——- : 2D complex array

Matrix for CNOT gate

E_DOWN(N, k, save=False)[source]¶

Defines matrix that projects k-th qubit on the state |↓〉≡ |1〉

Nint

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

kint

Position of the projection matrix in the tensor product.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

None.

: 2D complex array

Matrix |1〉〈1|_k of dimensions 2**N x 2**N

E_UP(N, k, save=False)[source]¶

Defines matrix that projects k-th qubit on the state |↑〉≡ |0〉

Nint

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

kint

Position of the projection matrix in the tensor product.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

None.

: 2D complex array

Matrix |0〉〈0|_k of dimensions 2**N x 2**N

PAULI_X(N, k, save=False)[source]¶

Defines a raising operator of the k-th qubit Parameters ———- N : int

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

kint

Position of the raising operators in the tensor product.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

: complex 2D array

PAULI_Y(N, k, save=False)[source]¶

Defines a raising operator of the k-th qubit Parameters ———- N : int

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

kint

Position of the raising operators in the tensor product.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

: complex 2D array

PAULI_Z(N, k, save=False)[source]¶

Defines a raising operator of the k-th qubit Parameters ———- N : int

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

kint

Position of the raising operators in the tensor product.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

: complex 2D array

RSWAP(N, k1, k2, save=False)[source]¶

Defines sqrt(SWAP) gate matrix for the qubits with the indices k1, k2.

Nint

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

k1, k2: int

Indices of the qubits.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

None. Returns ——- : 2D complex array

Matrix for SWAP gate

SIGMA_MINUS(N, k, save=False)[source]¶

Defines a lowering operator of the k-th qubit Parameters ———- N : int

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

kint

Position of the raising operators in the tensor product.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

: complex 2D array

The lowering operators X_k - i Y_k

SIGMA_PLUS(N, k, save=False)[source]¶

Defines a raising operator of the k-th qubit Parameters ———- N : int

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

kint

Position of the raising operators in the tensor product.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

: complex 2D array

The raising operators X_k + i Y_k

SIGMA_PRODUCT(N, k1, k2, save=False)[source]¶

Defines the dot product of two Pauli vectors.

Nint

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

k1, k2: int

Indices of the qubits.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

None. Returns ——- : 2D complex array

The inner product

ec{sigma_k1} cdot ec{sigma_k2}

SWAP(N, k1, k2, save=False)[source]¶

Defines SWAP gate matrix for the qubits with the indices k1, k2.

Nint

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

k1, k2: int

Indices of the qubits.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

None. Returns ——- : 2D complex array

Matrix for sqt(SWAP) gate

UNIT(N, save=False)[source]¶

Defines UNIT matrix of dimensions 2**N x 2**N

Nint

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operators.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

None.

: 2D complex array Unit matrix of dimensions 2**N x 2**N

add_operators(new_ops, disp=False)[source]¶

Add operator dictionary to an existing operator dictionary.

new_opsDictionary

Dictionary of operators to add to exisiting dictionary.

None.

check_name(filename=None)[source]¶

Check that a file name for an object file has the correct extention, and if not try to correct it or raise an error.

filename: string

String that indicates the name of the existing object file which contains a library of operators.

String with ‘.npz’ appended if no other file extension existed

check_ops(dict)[source]¶

Checks that a dictionary’s operator items are valid data structure types.

dictDictionary

Operator dictionary

None.

check_unitary(dict)[source]¶

Checks if a dictionary’s operator items are all unitary.

dictDictionary

Operator dictionary

None.

coded_op(save, **kwargs)[source]¶

Manages arbitrarily defined operators that were hard coded i.e. saves the operator to the operator library if specified in the operator function call and only computes the operator if it does not exist in the operator library. ———- func : function

An arbitrary function is sent.

op_keystr

Operator key name.

savebool

boolean indicator from original function call to save the operator to the library if true.

**kwargsdictionary
General structure: {‘var1’: val1, …, ‘varN’: valN,

‘struct’: stuct, ‘func’, func}

Contains variable names and values used to construct the operator, as well as, functions to help constuct the operator and operator library name.

: complex 2D array

The computed or loaded operator

construct(N, k, O, key_prefix=None)[source]¶

Creates matrix U_k of dimensions 2**N x 2**N for specified operator O

N: int

Number of 1-qubit degrees of freedom in the operator.

k: int

Position of the Pauli matrix in the tensor product.

O: 2D complex array key_prefix: string

optional key prefix name argument. Key structure is key_prefix_N#K#.

u_k: 2D complex array

Matrix U_k of dimensions 2**N x 2**N

keys()[source]¶
load_ops(filename, f_type=None, disp=False)[source]¶

Load the operator object file for later use.

filenameString

Name of the file with extension .npz for the operator object.

lib: Dictionary

Dictionary containing operators.

remove_operators(op_names, disp=False)[source]¶

Remove operator items from existing operator dictionary.

op_namesList

List of dictionary key strings that are to be removed from the existing dictionary.

None.

save_ops(filename=None)[source]¶

Save the operator dictionary in the current working directory as an object file for later use.

filenameString

Name of operator dictionary file. If the file type suffix is not included in the filename, it will be appended on to the filename string.

None.

qudipy.qutils.solvers module¶

Quantum utility solver functions

@author: simba

qudipy.qutils.solvers.build_1DSE_hamiltonian(consts, gparams)[source]¶

Build a single electron Hamilonian for the 1-dimensional potential specified in the gparams class. The laplacian operator is approximated by using a 1D 3-point stencil. The Hamilonian assumes a natural ordering format along the main diagonal.

constsConstants class

Contains constants value for material system.

gparamsGridParameters class

Contains grid and potential information

ham_1Dsparse 2D array

1-dimensional Hamtilonian. The diagonal elements are in natural ordering format

qudipy.qutils.solvers.build_2DSE_hamiltonian(consts, gparams)[source]¶

Build a single electron Hamilonian for the 2-dimensional potential specified in the gparams class. The laplacian operator is approximated by using a 2D 5-point stencil. The Hamiltonian assumes a natural ordering format along the main diagonal.

constsConstants class

Contains constants value for material system.

gparamsGridParameters class

Contains grid and potential information

ham_2Dsparse 2D array

2-dimensional Hamtilonian. The diagonal elements are in natural ordering format.

qudipy.qutils.solvers.solve_schrodinger_eq(consts, gparams, n_sols=1)[source]¶

Solve the time-independent Schrodinger-Equation H|Y> = E|Y> where H is the single-electron 1 (or 2)-dimensional Hamiltonian.

constsConstants class

Contains constants value for material system.

gparamsGridParameters class

Contains grid and potential information.

n_sols: int, optional

Number of eigenvectors and eigenenergies to return. The default is 1.

eig_enscomplex 1D array

Lowest eigenenergies sorted in ascending order.

eig_vecscomplex 2D array

Corresponding eigenvectors in natural order format. eig_vecs[:,i] is the eigenvector for eigenvalue eig_ens[i].

Module contents¶

Quantum utility helper module
